valuation.verdict: INCOMPLETE - Intrinsic value established at $36.50 (base case); MoS verdict requires current market price
valuation.reasoning: DCF valuation complete. Intrinsic value range: $31.48 (conservative, 3% growth) to $42.35 (optimistic, 7% growth), with base case of $36.50/share at 5% growth. Growth rate was capped at 5% per the quality score matrix: Business Quality of 24/40 falls below the 25/40 threshold, triggering CAGR × 0.60 rule with 5% ceiling. Financial health (35/50) and management quality (18/30) scores provide further context for the conservative approach. Buffett verdict (Buy/Watch/Hold/Avoid) and valuation score (0-10) cannot be determined without a verifiable current market price from SEC-authorized sources. The FCF fundamentals are strong ($1.25B in 2025, 14.9% CAGR), but the quality-adjusted growth assumption and 10% discount rate yield a fair value of approximately $36.50 per share.

qualityScoreSummary.businessQuality: 24/40 (60%) — genuine but narrow moat, inconsistent profitability history, AI disruption risk
qualityScoreSummary.managementQuality: 18/30 (60%) — debt elimination and acquisition discipline positive; no shareholder return program is a concern
qualityScoreSummary.financialHealth: 35/50 (70%) — debt-free and capital-light are strong; ROE below 15% and volatile margins are weak
qualityScoreSummary.discountRateRationale: 77/120 scaled to ~83/130 (standard business range 70-100/130) → 10% discount rate applied
